post_title: Give me your calmest and most gentle ambient recs
post_body:
Whatever you're thinking, make it twice as soothing. I don't want any kind of edge or dissonance or noise. Nothing complex or super layered. Just gentle, unassuming music that's barely there and perhaps a tad melancholic. I'm talking music for people that think even Music For Airports can be a bit perky at times!

Here is a good example: 

https://greyskylullabies.bandcamp.com/album/drones-and-swells
comments: Harold Budd. Everything by Budd is gentle & lovely while still being musically interesting. You can also say that  about Roger Eno’s piano works.
Such an incredible artist, so sad that he is no longer with us. This is my favourite of his: [https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=3Pjm0PmqnhE](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=3Pjm0PmqnhE)
I love Eno, but listening to a lot of his stuff makes me want to listen more intensely & deeply, if someone just wants to relax, it may be a bit 'busy' for them

Tetsu Inoue - Ambiant Otaku 

Stars of the Lid - Avec Laudenum

A Deeper Silence by Steve Roach

https://open.spotify.com/track/5ozJdSZWfmpnYg5DASHmF5?si=dHHwDbV2QJOpgU6qunajMw
This is the indisputable king. That the track length is written as ∞ on the back cover is 100% accurate. I once listened to this 24/7 (yes, also while sleeping) for 2 weeks straight.

I love that composition. There's really nothing like it. 

If you ever find yourself on 600 gamma of LSD and need something grounding but can't stand either "music" or silence then this is it.
Well said. His longform tracks are so good, got hooked when he started doing the Bloodmoon series, glad to own the Bloodmoon Rising CD set :)
Warmth
This
Eno, Thursday Afternoon. It's a song, it's an album😏
Amazing to drift away to...
For sure. A good one to just stare out the window to.
Chihei Hatakeyama - You’re Still in It
Excellent choice!
Got this one on cassette tape and it has become one of my faves by him!
Ooof jealous!!

I once released a cassette of Hakobune as well :) Maybe you know it already:

https://warmdronelabel.bandcamp.com/album/all-my-regret-for-nothing-remembered
Real nice thank you!
Hakobune: extremely soft, delicate, unforced drone. Often feels like it’s hovering just above silence
One of the most 'pure' and serene drone sounds that I know of. Own almost all of his (as well as Celer's) work, I know of very little that comes close to this level of drone serenity

P.S. I once released a cassette of Hakobune as well :) Maybe you know it already:

[https://warmdronelabel.bandcamp.com/album/all-my-regret-for-nothing-remembered](https://warmdronelabel.bandcamp.com/album/all-my-regret-for-nothing-remembered)

Everyone here is saying Stars of the Lid, which is valid. 

But check out Brian McBride's solo albums: "When the Detail Lost Its Freedom" and "Effective Disconnect" are amazing.

[Silent Motels](https://starsofthelid.bandcamp.com/track/silent-motels) is one of the most literal titles for an Ambient track ever. 




It is exactly what smoking a cig outside your door at a Days Inn off the highway at 3am sounds/feels like.
Try El Camino Real by William Basinski if you haven't yet. Also look into Bull of Heaven

I never miss an opportunity to recommend this. One of my favorite Ambient albums in recent memory:




[Yorkshire Modular Society - Moving](https://rohsrecords.bandcamp.com/album/moving?search_item_id=797084849&search_item_type=a&search_match_part=%3F&search_page_id=5349024299&search_page_no=0&search_rank=2&logged_in_mobile_menubar=true)
The collab with Brenden Eder Ensemble is OUTSTANDING. I/II/III
And you made yet another convert :) just listened to this for the first time, True bliss
Steve Roach - Quiet Music (especially the 3-hour version anniversary edition)

Stars of the lid; And The Refinement of Their Decline. 

For that matter, almost everything Stars of the Lid has produced!
Music for 9 Postcards by Hiroshi Yoshimura. My insomnia aid for years.
Splendid
Steve Roach's Still point, Long Thoughts, & A Deeper Silence might be what youre looking for
All of his longform tracks are exactly just that, indeed

Robert Rich - Nest.

Just the most serene feeling of warmth and gentle sounds, like laying in the sun.
Wow, I had not heard this wonderful album by him but after two days of listening it might become my favorite RR release. Thanks so much for sharing it
Glad you liked it, between that, Echo Of Small Things and Rudy Adrian’s “MoonWater” I’m covered for serene music.

Anything from Líom 

I personally love the “Gan tú” and “Oslo” albums.

Favorite song is Dusk
Excellent serene project!
*Six Songs for Invisible Gardens* by Green-House. One of my very favorites.

Oh and this one - Kevin Drumm, Just Lay Down and Forget It

[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=mfUT\_gQvr40&t=692s&pp=ygUbanVzdCBsYXkgZG93biBhbmQgZm9yZ2V0IGl0](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=mfUT_gQvr40&t=692s&pp=ygUbanVzdCBsYXkgZG93biBhbmQgZm9yZ2V0IGl0)
Kevin Drumm also made Tannenbaum which is very good
Love that Kevin Drumm album, haven't listened to it in ages. Will give it a spin again tonight, thanks for reminding me :)

Everything with Stars of the Lid. Avec Laudenum is a good (and short) starting point.


Biosphere and of course Brian Eno also comes to mind.

https://youtu.be/KT-mRoG9FmI?si=O-KvLhrE74wpuasf

Toshimi Mikami - Chi-Dance

These are all really relaxing to me, but a bit more than pure drone, sometimes pure drone makes me feel sad and apathic 🦦

https://open.spotify.com/track/1CdTax44yrr94P5rByPA9y?si=AjkEIYSlRwOtYiTQuCYB7Q

https://open.spotify.com/track/1dNX6lUalqsdphlkFDMNGm?si=GcOrWvwMTOele7T9BUiAXA

https://open.spotify.com/track/6R7txIpelw0ownnyWxaYzE?si=Z6dtA_rdSCaq1CcuaLGOuw

https://open.spotify.com/track/5yyMYO3trdJ4Mpq51PXMgB?si=SmDdDo1TQnCQ7Zvxrx9EOQ

https://open.spotify.com/track/1pXDFB8gCjU2eTAUKZqzpd?si=Ip5nsfCuR4iWjVIsihDf_A
Halftribe is excellent 😊
Amazing artist, he is sorely missed
Brian Eno - Neroli

Lullatone might be up your alley. It's in their name! A couple of favorite tracks:

* Falling Asleep With a Book on Your Chest
* A Blanket of Snow Spotted From Under the Blankets
Stephan Mathieu - remain
Very nice, thanks for sharing!
Gentle mmmbient rainy dub-techno. [https://yagya.bandcamp.com/album/rigning](https://yagya.bandcamp.com/album/rigning)

https://youtu.be/OqoiZ80Mklo?si=8TtkDUStE3r14Fsu

This is a lesser-known Aphex Twin track that pays tribute to his late father. It’s become one of my favorite ambient tracks of his as of late.

I have a whole playlist for that:

https://open.spotify.com/playlist/72yl1RhUsMT8Jmeby4Eocx

this one is my favorite drone of all time, although may be even too calm if compared with other songs here. it's basically one chord with additional atmospheric sounds once in a while  
[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=jxJ81L8ux4c](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=jxJ81L8ux4c)
https://astrangelyisolatedplace.bandcamp.com/album/twenty-three-and-twenty-six
I meditate to this a lot. It is beautifully calming.
Robert Rich - Somnium.  7 *hours* long.
https://icterinedrones.bandcamp.com/track/passing-through-an-anxious-world

Katsura - Autumn Sunshine \[北風 variation\]  
Ed Herbers - Gravity Well  
Motionfield - Always There part 5
Moshimoss
Chuck Johnson
Solar Fields - Until We Meet The Sky
Self-promotion is not permitted outside of the weekly thread.

Alexandre Centeio - Movanta 

https://open.spotify.com/track/3BvEGz0TCo1WYLXXwWtGjC?si=K1M91NlvRGqDrACm07WpTg

I love the band Telomere. I listen to a playlist of all their albums over and over. Lux Primordia, The Stellar Sea, Zoetosis, Supergiant, Astral Currents. I love it all. 

As someone else said, I don't like drone music; I want my music to have a little movement even when it's more atmospheric.
Folding In On Itself by Ezekiel Honig (2011)
Cage 4’33”
The Deep Skies albums by Kevin Kendle.
Grapes from the Estate - Oren Ambarchi
This sounds like an AI/bot.
Ouch. I'm not 😢

QTT4 By Huerco S.
Pour Homme By Stewart Bird (bandcamp)
Superbath By Special Guest DJ
Scene By Chihei Hatakeyama
Esto te puede interesar  https://open.spotify.com/track/5RWL85vQNg5VcYHc7tExSx?si=yCYAqiwjRcetlIAQVJWHIg

I did an online radio show featuring only this kind of drone music for 3 years. If you like, you can check out the last 10 sets here, thats 20 hours of what I call "warm drone" for ya:

https://www.mixcloud.com/casper-van-der-veen/
Anthene - Virtue
